metadata.name: Military Retirement System Rules
metadata.description: Comprehensive rules for all four military retirement systems (Final Pay, High-3, REDUX/CSB, BRS), Chapter 61 disability retirement, SBP, and concurrent receipt (CRDP/CRSC) with tax treatment and election logic. Covers all eight uniformed services including USPHS and NOAA Corps.
sources: 10 U.S.C. § 1414 — Concurrent Retirement and Disability Pay (CRDP)
Congressional Research Service IF10594 (Concurrent Receipt primer)
DoD Financial Management Regulation, Volume 7B, Chapter 3
10 U.S.C. §101(a)(4) — Definition of uniformed services (all eight)
FY2021 NDAA — Coast Guard inclusion in Military Retirement Fund
notes: Military retirement system is determined by date of initial entry into military service (DIEMS).
All systems require 20+ years of active duty service for immediate retirement (or 20 qualifying years for reserve retirement).
Disability retirement available with fewer than 20 years under different rules.
Military retired pay is taxable as ordinary income at the federal level.
Chapter 61 disability retirees may have different formulas, concurrent receipt eligibility, and TRICARE cost structures than longevity retirees.
These retirement systems apply to all eight uniformed services: Army, Marine Corps, Navy, Air Force, Space Force, Coast Guard, USPHS Commissioned Corps, and NOAA Commissioned Officer Corps.
USPHS and NOAA Corps officers use the same pay table (military-pay-tables.json) and the same retirement formulas as DoD service members.
USPHS (~6,000 active officers) and NOAA Corps (~350 active officers) retirement pay is processed by the U.S. Coast Guard Pay and Personnel Center (PPC).

Critical funding difference: DoD and Coast Guard retired pay comes from the Military Retirement Fund (protected during shutdowns). USPHS retired pay comes from HHS appropriations and NOAA from Commerce appropriations — both are vulnerable to payment disruption during government shutdowns (missed October 2025 payments).

BRS opt-in was available to all seven uniformed services (eight minus Space Force, which did not yet exist) from January 1 through December 31, 2018. All new USPHS accessions on or after January 1, 2018 are automatically enrolled in BRS.

retirement_systems.status: Legacy — very few active members remain
retirement_systems.cola_rule: Full CPI-W (same as Social Security COLA)

retirement_systems.applies_to: DIEMS on or after September 8, 1980 and before January 1, 2018 (who did not opt into BRS)
retirement_systems.pay_base: Average of highest 36 months of basic pay
retirement_systems.cola_rule: Full CPI-W (same as Social Security COLA)

example.description: 20 years × 2.5% = 50% of high-3 average basic pay

retirement_systems.applies_to: DIEMS on or after August 1, 1986 and before January 1, 2018. Must have elected CSB at 15 years of service.
retirement_systems.status: Legacy — option eliminated for those entering after 2018
retirement_systems.formula: high_3_average × (2.5% - 1% reduction for each year under 30) × years_of_service
retirement_systems.multiplier_note: Effective 2.0% per year at 20 years due to REDUX formula, but catches up to 2.5% at 30 years
retirement_systems.pay_base: Average of highest 36 months of basic pay
retirement_systems.cola_rule: CPI-W minus 1% (partial COLA — purchasing power erodes over time)
retirement_systems.cola_catchup: One-time recomputation to full CPI at age 62, then COLA-1% resumes
retirement_systems.csb_note: $30,000 Career Status Bonus paid at 15 years in exchange for accepting REDUX formula

example.description: 20 years × 2.0% effective = 40% of high-3 (vs 50% under High-3 system)

retirement_systems.applies_to: DIEMS on or after January 1, 2018. Optional opt-in was available in 2018 for those with fewer than 12 years of service.
retirement_systems.pay_base: Average of highest 36 months of basic pay
retirement_systems.cola_rule: Full CPI-W (same as Social Security COLA)

tsp_match_details.automatic_contribution_note: DoD contributes 1% of basic pay regardless of member contribution
tsp_match_details.vesting: 2 years of service for automatic 1%; matching vests immediately
tsp_match_details.notes: Maximum DoD contribution is 5% of basic pay (1% auto + 4% match on 5% member contribution)

continuation_pay_details.notes: Amount varies by service branch and occupational specialty

lump_sum_option.notes: Member can elect to receive 25% or 50% of retired pay as lump sum at retirement, with reduced monthly annuity until age 67 (SS FRA), then full amount resumes

example.description: 20 years × 2.0% = 40% of high-3 average (lower than High-3 system, but offset by TSP matching)

reserve_retirement.eligibility: 20 qualifying years of service (must have 50+ retirement points per year)
reserve_retirement.early_pay_provision: Age reduced by 90 days for each 90 days of active duty in a fiscal year after January 28, 2008 (earliest age 50)
reserve_retirement.notes: Reserve/Guard retirement pay begins at age 60 (or earlier per early pay provision). Points-based system.

survivor_benefit_plan.description: SBP provides up to 55% of retired pay to eligible survivors
survivor_benefit_plan.base_amount_options: Full retired pay or any lesser amount (minimum $300/month)
survivor_benefit_plan.social_security_offset: Eliminated by NDAA FY2023 — SBP-DIC offset fully repealed as of 2023
survivor_benefit_plan.paid_up_provision: Premiums stop after 360 months of payments AND reaching age 70

concurrent_receipt.overview: Prior to 2004, military retirees had retired pay reduced dollar-for-dollar by VA disability compensation amount. CRDP and CRSC partially restore this.

va_offset_rule.description: Default rule: military retired pay is reduced by VA disability compensation amount
va_offset_rule.applies_to: All military retirees receiving VA disability compensation, unless CRDP or CRSC applies
va_offset_rule.tax_effect: The offset portion becomes tax-free (replaced by tax-free VA comp). Remaining retired pay is taxable.

crdp.name: Concurrent Retirement and Disability Pay

eligibility.years_of_service: 20+ years (longevity retirement or Chapter 61 with 20+ years)
eligibility.reserve_note: Age 60+ (or earlier per early pay provision) with 20 qualifying years

not_eligible: Chapter 61 retirees with fewer than 20 years of service
Longevity retirees with VA rating of 40% or less (non-combat)
crdp.effect: Full military retired pay restored — no VA offset. Retiree receives BOTH full retired pay and full VA compensation.

computation.restored_amount: Amount that was previously offset (the lesser of retired pay and VA comp amount)
computation.chapter_61_cap: For Chapter 61 with 20+ years: CRDP only restores up to the longevity-based retired pay amount. Any disability-based retired pay above the longevity amount remains subject to offset.

tax_treatment.retired_pay: Taxable as ordinary income (full amount restored)
tax_treatment.crdp_payment: Taxable — CRDP is considered restored retired pay, not VA compensation
tax_treatment.net_effect: Higher gross income but also higher tax liability than CRSC

crdp.application_note: DFAS determines CRDP automatically. No application needed.

crsc.name: Combat-Related Special Compensation

eligibility.years_of_service: Any (including Chapter 61 with fewer than 20 years, per 2008 NDAA)
eligibility.disability_must_be: Combat-related (actual combat, hazardous duty, simulating war, instrumentality of war)

combat_related_categories.description: Direct Combat — injuries from enemy action or hostile fire
Hazardous Duty — injuries during inherently dangerous military duties
Simulating War — injuries during training exercises simulating combat conditions
Instrumentality of War — injuries caused by military vehicles, weapons, equipment, or munitions

crsc.effect: Tax-free payment replaces some or all of the VA offset to retired pay

computation.description: CRSC equals the VA compensation rate for combat-related disabilities only, capped at the retired pay offset amount

chapter_61_special_rule.description: For Chapter 61 retirees: CRSC is reduced by the difference between disability-based retired pay and longevity-based retired pay
chapter_61_special_rule.formula: crsc_amount = min(va_comp_for_combat_disabilities, retired_pay_offset) - max(0, disability_retired_pay - longevity_retired_pay)
chapter_61_special_rule.notes: This prevents Chapter 61 retirees from receiving more than they would have earned through longevity alone

computation.partial_combat_note: If only some disabilities are combat-related, CRSC is based on the VA compensation rate for the combat-related portion only, not the total VA rating

tax_treatment.net_effect: Lower gross income but lower tax liability — may result in higher net income than CRDP depending on marginal tax rate

crdp_vs_crsc_election.description: Retirees eligible for both CRDP and CRSC must choose one — cannot receive both simultaneously
crdp_vs_crsc_election.election_mechanism: Annual open season administered by DFAS

decision_factors.guidance: Higher marginal tax rate favors CRSC (tax-free) over CRDP (taxable)

decision_factors.factor: Combat-related rating vs total VA rating
decision_factors.guidance: If combat-related rating equals total VA rating, CRSC and CRDP may be similar amounts. If combat-related rating is lower than total, CRDP likely pays more gross.
MFJ with high household income may favor CRSC. Single filer with lower income may favor CRDP.
In states that tax military retired pay, CRSC advantage increases (since CRSC is also state-tax-free)
CRSC does not count as income for means-tested programs (ACA subsidies, Medicaid, etc.). CRDP does.

crdp_vs_crsc_election.auto_selection: If CRSC is more beneficial than CRDP, DFAS will automatically elect CRSC for the retiree

no_concurrent_receipt_group.description: Retirees who still face full dollar-for-dollar offset with no CRDP or CRSC relief

group_1.description: Longevity retirees (20+ years) with VA rating of 40% or less and NO combat-related disabilities
group_1.count_estimate: Significant population — often called the 'forgotten veterans'
group_1.legislative_status: Multiple bills introduced to close this gap (e.g., Major Richard Star Act for Chapter 61)

group_2.description: Chapter 61 retirees (<20 years) with non-combat disabilities
group_2.notes: Eligible for CRSC only if disabilities are combat-related. No CRDP eligibility regardless.

tax_exclusion_for_disability_retired_pay.description: Separate from CRDP/CRSC: military disability retired pay itself may be partially or fully tax-exempt under IRS rules
tax_exclusion_for_disability_retired_pay.irs_rule: Under 26 U.S.C. § 104(a)(4) and § 104(b)(3), disability retired pay is tax-free to the extent of the VA disability rating percentage
tax_exclusion_for_disability_retired_pay.applies_to: Chapter 61 disability retirees and retirees who receive disability retired pay
tax_exclusion_for_disability_retired_pay.combat_injured_exclusion: Members who received disability retired pay and were in a combat zone may exclude the entire amount
tax_exclusion_for_disability_retired_pay.interaction_with_crdp_crsc: This exclusion applies independently. A retiree receiving CRDP may still have a portion of their base retired pay excluded if they qualify under § 104.

disability_retirement.eligibility: Member with permanent, stable medical condition rated 30%+ by service branch Physical Evaluation Board (PEB)

temporary_disability_retired_list.description: TDRL — placed on temporary list if condition not stable; re-evaluated every 18 months for up to 5 years

permanent_disability_retired_list.description: PDRL — permanent retirement when condition is stable and rated 30%+

formula.description: Retired pay is the GREATER of the two methods below

method_1_disability.name: Disability percentage method
method_1_disability.notes: DoD disability rating (not VA rating) is used for this calculation

method_2_longevity.name: Longevity method (years of service)
method_2_longevity.notes: Same formula as standard 20-year retirement, but applied at fewer years

minimum_retired_pay_floor.description: If rated 30%+, minimum retired pay cannot be less than 30% × pay base (for those with very few years of service)

severance_alternative.description: Members rated below 30% by PEB receive disability severance pay (lump sum), not retired pay
severance_alternative.taxability: Tax-free if disability is combat-related; taxable otherwise
severance_alternative.notes: Not eligible for military retired pay, SBP, or TRICARE retiree coverage

disability_retirement.cola_rule: Same as the member's underlying retirement system (based on DIEMS)
disability_retirement.tricare_eligibility: TRICARE retiree coverage applies (Prime or Select). Medically retired members receive frozen enrollment fees — see tricare-rates.json medically_retired_fee_schedule
disability_retirement.sbp_eligibility: Eligible for Survivor Benefit Plan on same terms as longevity retirees
